data.title: Aebi Schmidt Group, a specialty vehicles leader listed on Nasdaq (AEBI), pays its first dividend.
data.description: A global leader in specialty vehicles, Aebi Schmidt Group (NASDAQ: AEBI) has confirmed its first dividend payout in Frauenfeld, Switzerland on September 26, 2025.

data.title: Leo Cancer Care and Sumitomo Heavy Industries partner for Proton Therapy; new configuration to revolutionize cancer care.
data.description: Middleton, Wisconsin - On September 26, 2025, Leo Cancer Care, a prominent provider of advanced cancer treatments, partnered with Sumitomo Heavy Industries, a leading developer of medical technology worldwide, to launch a revolutionary Proton Therapy setup. The collaboration marks a significant advancement in cancer care solutions and highlights the ongoing commitment to enhance medical technologies for improved patient outcomes.

data.title: RIMAN receives 20-year USDA protection for Giant BYoungPool™, revolutionizing K-beauty.oweringCentella asiatica variety.
data.description: In a press release dated September 26, 2025, from Seoul, South Korea, RIMAN, a prominent figure in K-beauty and Direct Sales, revealed a significant development in the beauty industry. The company disclosed that its exclusive Centella asiatica strain, known as Giant BYoungPool™, secured a 20-year approval. This innovation showcases the fusion of K-beauty heritage with cutting-edge skincare advancements, further solidifying RIMAN's position as a pioneer in the beauty sector.

data.title: White Cap opens first Enterprise Distribution Center in Perris, California. Leading distributor of construction supplies.
data.description: On September 26, 2025, in Atlanta, White Cap, a top distributor of specialized construction supplies and safety products for professional contractors, marked the inauguration of its initial Enterprise Distribution Center (EDC) in Perris, California. This significant milestone signifies White Cap's commitment to expanding its operations and presence in the industry.

data.title: Bodily injury costs rise in Nevada, prompting Mercury Insurance to offer new umbrella policy to protect personal wealth.
data.description: Mercury Insurance has introduced a new umbrella insurance policy aimed at providing enhanced protection for the personal wealth of Nevada residents against bodily injury claims. This initiative comes amidst rising occurrences of multi-vehicle accidents, dog attacks, and injuries to individuals such as delivery personnel on private properties. Through this new policy, Mercury aims to offer additional coverage and financial security to individuals facing unforeseen events resulting in bodily harm.

data.title: Survey: 48% of crypto investors prioritize Bitcoin in portfolios post-Halving. Learn how traders strategize.
data.description: A recent survey conducted by CoinGecko shows that nearly half, or 48%, of cryptocurrency investors give priority to Bitcoin in their investment portfolios, especially following the recent Halving event. The survey delves into the diverse strategies being adopted by traders and investors as they position themselves in the market.
Morgan Stanley increased the target price for Microsoft from $582.00 to $625.00, indicating a more optimistic outlook

data.title: Bybit partners with Plasma to list XPL, enabling zero-fee USDT transfers. Bybit is the world's 2nd largest exchange.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on September 26th, 2025, Bybit, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange based on trading volume, has announced a significant partnership with Plasma, a cutting-edge Layer 1 blockchain tailored for stablecoin transactions. This collaboration signifies a major development as Bybit continues to expand its presence in the crypto space.

data.title: LightPath Technologies Q4 loss wider than expected at 16 cents per share, revenue beats expectations at $12.21 million.
data.description: LightPath Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: LPTH) announced a fourth-quarter loss of 16 cents per share, exceeding analyst predictions of four cents loss. Despite this wider-than-expected loss, the quarterly revenue reached $12.21 million, slightly surpassing the Street's estimate of $12.2 million. This marks a significant increase from the previous year's revenue of $8.63 million for the same quarter. The company expressed confidence in the strategic shift away from Germanium optics towards expanding their proprietary BlackDiamond glass in defense applications as the key focus in Fiscal 2025. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: 7 top public companies are now holding billions of dollars in Ethereum, making them the largest ETH investors.
data.description: Major companies trading in the stock market are increasingly acquiring substantial amounts of Ethereum, bringing in a significant influx of billions of dollars in ETH. These entities have become the top holders of the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Costco beats Q4 revenue estimates with $86.16 billion, EPS of $5.87 surpasses $5.80. Membership fees rise to $1.72 billion.
data.description: Costco Wholesale Corp (NASDAQ: COST) released its fiscal fourth-quarter 2025 financial results, surpassing expectations. The company reported Q4 revenue of $86.16 billion, exceeding analyst projections of $86.12 billion. Additionally, Costco's adjusted fourth-quarter earnings stood at $5.87 per share, outperforming estimates of $5.80 per share. Membership fees notably rose to around $1.72 billion from $1.51 billion in the same period last year. In September 2024, Costco initiated its inaugural membership fee increase of $5. For more details, the full article is accessible via Benzinga.com.

data.title: Qlarant Foundation awarded $450,000 grants to charities; Program provided 16 grants of up to $50,000 each.
data.description: The 2025 Program, organized by the Qlarant Foundation in Easton, Md., awarded a total of 16 separate grants during its annual Grantee Celebration on September 24, 2025. Each grant provided up to $50,000 to nonprofit organizations. The event took place at the foundation's headquarters and aimed to recognize the contributions of these 16 grantees.

data.title: Desert Harvest launches human-identical Lactoferrin for menopause supported by effera™ protein.
data.description: Helaina Biotech has selected Desert Harvest to introduce effera™, a unique milk protein supporting hormone, immune, and vaginal health, to the menopause industry. The collaboration, announced on September 26, 2025, in Colorado Springs, marks a pioneering move in the market. Desert Harvest, known for its expertise in pelvic health, is set to leverage this breakthrough ingredient to offer innovative solutions.

data.title: Youxin Technology Ltd faces Nasdaq delisting due to low stock price. Plans to appeal. 1-for-80 share consolidation announced.
data.description: Youxin Technology Ltd, a SaaS and PaaS provider based in Guangzhou, China, received a staff determination notice from Nasdaq on September 22, 2025, stating the delisting of the Company's securities under Listing Rule 5810(c)(3)(A)(iii). The reason cited was the closing bid price of the Company’s Class A ordinary shares being below $0.10 for ten consecutive trading days, a violation known as the "Low Priced Stocks Rule". Unless an appeal is made, the securities will be suspended from trading on Nasdaq at the opening of business on October 1, 2025. This delisting notice could potentially lead to the filing of Form 25-NSE with the SEC, resulting in the removal of the Company’s securities from the Nasdaq Stock Market.

data.title: Morgan Stanley to send out participation notifications.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the global economy is expected to grow by approximately 4.2% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Advanced economies are projected to grow by 5.1% this year, while emerging markets and developing economies are anticipated to grow by 6.0%. Despite the overall positive outlook, uncertainties surrounding vaccine distribution, virus mutations, and uneven economic recoveries across regions could pose risks to this forecast. Additionally, global trade volumes are estimated to have declined by about 9.2% in 2020, with a projected recovery of 7.2% in 2021. The report emphasizes the importance of continued policy support and international cooperation to ensure a sustainable and inclusive recovery for all countries.
